batch_summary.note: Oct 2025: 1 RF-11 primary flag. S002 — fear relay parentification. Arc→WATCHING (2 signals).

primary_flags.cross_rf: RF-02 (visit-refusal cascade, B-028 COMP-RF02 signals); RF-04 (B-028 COMP-RF04-026 'so afraid of you' cluster)
primary_flags.message_text: ROW-14490: 'Why are you telling her she has to stay at your house when she wants to come home because the judge said so' | ROW-14508: 'She doesn\'t feel safe and supported as evidenced by the texts I got' | ROW-14517: contextual fear-framing cluster concludes | Related: 'she got told not to say anything cause she\'s so afraid of you'
primary_flags.summary: Oct 2025: Melissa deploys Olivia's reported fear as a systematic visit-refusal justification. Multi-mechanism RF-11 signal in the Oct 4-7 child-fear-relay cluster: (1) ROW-14490: 'the judge said so' — court order weaponized as a coercive tool; Eli is framed as using legal authority to threaten the child ('making her stay because the judge said so') which inverts the legal obligation into a child-endangerment framing. This directly involves Olivia's understanding of the court order in the adult legal conflict. (2) ROW-14508: 'She doesn't feel safe and supported as evidenced by the texts I got' — Olivia's texts to Melissa during Eli's parenting time deployed as evidence against Eli; Olivia placed in the role of evidence-gatherer and safety-determinator. (3) Fear relay cluster: Olivia's emotional state (afraid, unsafe, doesn't want to go) is systematically relayed and amplified as adult-conflict justification. 'She got told not to say anything cause she's so afraid of you' — conditioning Olivia's expression around fear. ADD-002 context: Olivia as safety-determinator for visit compliance. ADD-006 context: fear relay as stabilizer mechanism — Melissa uses Olivia's reported fear as emotional leverage. Compound COMP-RF11-002: RF-11+RF-02+RF-04.
